MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Emilie Calhoun (in person) for Kimberly Rhine Gagosian and Ronald Gagosian. Armenak Kavcioglu for Lydia Rhine.

The Court gave a general advisement that parties present in the courtroom are adhering to social distancing and wear a face covering, that this hearing is being conducted by Zoom and the Court Call line has been activated to allow the public to listen in.

The Court stated that this is on calendar for motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication and that the Court released a tentative ruling. Ms. Calhoun stated that she contested the tenative ruling, requested clarification on the tentative ruling and presented her oral argument in suport of her postion. Mr. Kavcioglu argued his position regarding undue influence and provided case background.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative rulingin full: Respondent's Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative, Motion for Summary Adjudication to the Second Amended Petition to Contest the Validity of the Challenged First and Second Sets of Amendments to the Ernest V. Rhine Declaration of Trust, UDT April 9, 2000, on the Grounds of Undue Influence, filed in In The Ernest V. Rhine Trust (PTR-18-301983). The pleadings define the issues to be considered on a motion for summary judgment. (Sadlier v. Superior Court (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 1050, 1055; Benedek v. PLC Santa Monica, LLC (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 1351, 1355-56). The Second Amended Petition's claims and causes of action include: (1) that the First Amendment to the Trust (September 14, 2004) was procured through undue influence, as defined in Probate Code section 86 and Welf. & Inst Code section 15610.70; and (2) that the Second Amendment to the Trust (March 19, 2005) was procured through undue influence, as defined in Probate Code section 86 and Welf. & Inst Code section 15610.70.
Respondent's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Respondent's Motion for Summary Adjudication in the alternative ("MSA") is also DENIED. As explained below, triable issues of fact exist as to one or more causes of action and, therefore, Respondent is not entitled to judgment as a matter of law. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 437c(c).)
The Court GRANTS Respondent's request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)
The Court GRANTS Petitioner's request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)
The Court OVERRULES both Petitioner's and Respondent's evidentiary objections.
A party moving for summary judgment bears the "initial burden of production to make a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact." (Falcon v. Long Beach Genetics, Inc. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1263, 1271). "A defendant moving for summary judgment is entitled to summary judgment if he or she either conclusively negates an element of the plaintiff's cause of action, or shows that the plaintiff cannot establish at least one element of the cause of action." (Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 176, citing Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 853.)

Judge Ross C. Moody, Staff Attorney: Elif Sonmez-Kirsch. Clerk: N. Wong, Court Reporter: Liz Lee. CSR: 5828. Reported. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado.(D204)

Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David K. Ries for Adult Protective Services (respondent). David Parnall for Kimberly Gagosian (moving party). Appearing via Courtcall Larry Larson for Lydia Rhine (objector).

Mr. Ries reported that he and Mr. Parnall met and conferred after the tentative ruling was released and stipulated to an order that would allow APS to produce documents directly to Mr. Parnall's office without having the file those documents under seal with the court. Mr. Parnall provided oral argument in support of his request for production of documents. Mr. Larson stated that they would produce the documents limited to the time frame of the marriage. The Court stated that the scope of the production will go back 10 years. Further more the Court informed parties in regards to further discovery brief issues, the parties may send a letter to the Court and then the Court and counsel may have a phone conference instead of filing lengthy motions.

The Court adopted the modified tentative ruling in Kimberly Gagosian's Motion to Compel Production of Records Held by San Francisco Human Services Agency and granted the stipulated order granting the motion to compel production of records held by San Francisco Human Services Agency (order for the same signed in open court).

The Court adopted the modified tentative ruling of Kimberly Rhine Gagosian's ("Respondent") Motion to Compel Petitioner Lydia Rhine's ("Petitioner") Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents ("RFPs") is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Mr. Parnall to prepare a form of order that conforms to the modified tentative.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling in Respondent Kimberly Rhine Gagosian's ("Respondent") Motion to Compel Petitioner Lydia Rhine's ("Petitioner") Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents ("RFPs") is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Mr. Parnall to prepare a form of order that conforms to the tentaive.
